task_completion.description: Agent uses the backup header option to mount the corrupted volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the VeraCrypt GUI, the 'Use backup header embedded in a volume if available' option is checked in Mount Options, OR in the terminal, the `veracrypt` command is executed with the `--backup-header` flag.

task_completion.description: The volume is successfully mounted at the specified mount point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the volume mounted at `/home/ga/MountPoints/slot1` (e.g., via `lsblk`, `mount`, or `ls /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1`), or the VeraCrypt GUI showing the volume mounted at that path.

task_completion.description: Agent computes the SHA-256 checksums of the three files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing `sha256sum` commands executed on `project_ssh_config`, `infrastructure_hosts`, and `quarterly_metrics.csv`.

task_completion.description: The recovery report is created at the correct path with the required header and footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the creation or editing of `/home/ga/recovery_report.txt` with 'RECOVERY REPORT' on the first line and 'RECOVERY COMPLETE: 3 files verified' on the last line.

task_completion.description: The recovery report contains the correct checksum format for the three files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `/home/ga/recovery_report.txt` show the checksums with two spaces and the filename (without path) for each of the three files.

integrity.description: Agent actually computed the checksums of the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the agent running `sha256sum` on the actual files inside the mounted volume, rather than hallucinating or pasting pre-computed hashes.

integrity.description: Agent used VeraCrypt to mount the volume.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the VeraCrypt GUI or the `veracrypt` CLI tool, rather than attempting to bypass encryption using unauthorized tools.

integrity.description: The report was actually saved to the filesystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the file being written to disk, such as a successful save in a text editor (e.g., nano) or a `cat /home/ga/recovery_report.txt` command showing the final contents, rather than just printing the text to stdout.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Volume path: /home/ga/Volumes/corrupted_volume.hc. Mount point: /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1. Expected files: project_ssh_config, infrastructure_hosts, quarterly_metrics.csv. Report path: /home/ga/recovery_report.txt.
